summary: Alfred is an architecturally intentional Rust text editor with a clear functional-core / imperative-shell split enforced at crate boundaries. The codebase is well-documented at the decision level (8 ADRs, walkthrough docs, feature architecture docs) and module-level (comprehensive Rustdoc on every module and public function). The main comprehensibility risk is the size concentration in two files (app.rs ~8000 lines, bridge.rs ~5200 lines) that act as complexity hotspots. The absence of a README at the repo root is a significant discoverability gap for new contributors.

top_hotspots.notes: Most frequently changed file in recent git history (browse-mode, keybinding, operator logic). Contains ~8000 lines mixing event loop, key conversion, operator dispatch, motion resolution, and command execution. High McCabe complexity from nested match arms.

~5200 lines of Lisp primitive registrations. Each primitive is a closure capturing shared mutable state. Changes to the bridge require understanding closure lifetimes and RefCell borrowing rules. Recently extended with filesystem primitives.

~3350 lines. The EditorState struct itself (40+ public fields) is the single most coupled type in the system -- any feature addition touches this file. Recent additions: panels, line_styles, focused_panel, cli_argument.

recommendations.title: Add a root README.md
recommendations.description: The repository has no README at the project root. This is the primary entry point for new contributors and is checked by every code hosting platform and automated tool. A README should cover: project purpose, quickstart, crate overview, and link to the ADRs and walkthrough docs.

recommendations.title: Decompose app.rs into focused modules
recommendations.description: alfred-tui/src/app.rs is ~8000 lines and contains the event loop, key conversion, operator/motion dispatch, and all key handling logic. Split into at minimum: event_loop.rs (I/O), key_dispatch.rs (pure key handling), operator.rs (operator/motion logic), and command_execution.rs. This would also allow moving operator/motion domain logic back into alfred-core where it belongs.

recommendations.title: Introduce domain event types to replace stringly-typed hooks
recommendations.description: The HookRegistry uses string hook names (e.g. 'render-gutter-hook', 'on-save'). Define a typed HookEvent enum or at minimum constants for hook names in alfred-core to prevent typos, enable compile-time discovery, and make hook contracts explicit.

recommendations.title: Move InputState and Operator enums to alfred-core
recommendations.description: InputState and Operator are defined in alfred-tui/src/app.rs but represent editor domain concepts (modal editing state, operator-pending state). Moving them to alfred-core would allow unit testing operator/motion logic without the TUI layer and clarify the domain model.

recommendations.title: Decompose EditorState into sub-structs
recommendations.description: EditorState has 40+ public fields covering unrelated concerns: editing state (buffer, cursor, viewport), modal state (mode, selection_start, visual_line_mode), macro state (macro_recording, macro_buffer, macro_registers), search state, jump/change lists, and rendering state (line_styles, panels). Grouping into sub-structs (ModalState, MacroState, SearchState, etc.) would reduce the coupling surface and make each concern independently understandable.
